mnemonics: B.I.N.S. (Bernoulli Independent Number Success) - Binomial is just a sum of n independent Bernoulli trials with probability p of success.
Mean is 'n-p' (sounds like 'emp'), Variance is 'n-p-q' (sounds like 'empty')
keyPoints: The mean (expected value) of a Binomial distribution B(n, p) is μ = n * p.
The variance of a Binomial distribution B(n, p) is σ² = n * p * (1 - p), often denoted as n * p * q where q = 1 - p.
The distribution models the number of successes in a fixed number of independent trials, each with the same probability of success.
commonMistakes: Confusing the formulas for mean (n*p) and variance (n*p*q) and using one for the other.
Forgetting that the trials must be independent for the Binomial model to be valid.
Using the Binomial distribution when the number of trials is not fixed (use Geometric or Negative Binomial instead).
